checks.name: schema_completeness
checks.detail: All required payload fields present. Includes customSections (priority_mapping, weekly_cadence, notion_database_schema) — bonus content.

checks.name: flow_integrity
checks.detail: 3 flows: flow_monday_planning (13 nodes/14 edges), flow_friday_archival (12 nodes/13 edges), flow_midweek_sync (9 nodes/9 edges). All well-connected with error handling.

checks.name: trigger_validity
checks.detail: 2 schedule triggers: 0 7 * * 1 (Monday 7 AM) and 0 17 * * 5 (Friday 5 PM). Both valid cron.

checks.name: adoption_question_fitness
checks.detail: 5 adoption_questions added: notion_database_id, todoist_project_id, slack_channel, planning_time, archive_time

checks.name: safety_scan
checks.detail: 0 critical findings. No destructive operations. Notion updates are status changes, not deletions.

dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity 60+ words. Instructions cover 2 major cycles (Monday planning with 6 steps, Friday archival with 6 steps). Tool guidance covers Notion, Todoist, Slack APIs with specific endpoints and query params. 3 examples (normal planning, archival, empty week). Error handling covers all services. Bonus customSections for priority mapping and database schema.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: All tools mapped: http_request for all 3 APIs, file_read/file_write for state persistence. Notion query with filter/sort, Todoist task creation with priority mapping, Slack Block Kit posting. No phantom tools.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: Monday 7 AM and Friday 5 PM triggers exactly match the dual-cycle cadence described in the prompt. Mid-week sync is user_message triggered, also documented.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: All 3 service_flow entries fully backed by connectors with complete credential fields. Notion integration_token, Todoist api_token, Slack bot_token all documented.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: State file (state/weekly_plan_{YYYY_WW}.json) tracks Notion-to-Todoist mappings across Monday-Friday cycle. Agent memory for completion patterns mentioned but not deeply structured.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Covers Notion 401/404/429, Todoist 403/creation failures/duplicate detection, Slack channel_not_found/message limits, state file corruption/missing. Partial failure handling with continued processing.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: All 3 flows fully covered: Monday planning, Friday archival, and mid-week manual sync. Step-by-step procedures match flow diagrams precisely.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Clear outcome: automates weekly planning overhead. Bidirectional state between Notion and Todoist with team communication. Concrete time savings for project managers.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: Notion, Todoist, Slack APIs are standard. Priority mapping (P0-P3 to Todoist priorities) is well-defined. State file approach is practical. Notion database schema requirements are clearly documented.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Priority elevation for imminent deadlines, dependency detection, completion tracking with archival — adds reasoning beyond what Zapier/n8n could easily replicate. Mid-week sync adds flexibility.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: All 5 questions target essential config. database_id and project_id are required. Time preferences allow schedule customization.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Good defaults for channel (#weekly-planning), times (7 AM, 5 PM). notion_database_id and todoist_project_id must be user-provided. User needs 2 values to start.

issues: No adoption_questions existed — 5 added covering Notion database, Todoist project, Slack channel, and schedule preferences
Notion and Todoist connectors may not have builtin definitions

template: Added 5 adoption_questions: notion_database_id, todoist_project_id, slack_channel, planning_time, archive_time
Included use_case_ids on planning_time and archive_time to scope them to their respective cycle flows
Used select type with time options for schedule customization
